grantor_names: W. W. Holding and wife, Josephine H. Holding
document_extractions.legal_description_full: BEGINNING at a stake on the North Side of Fort Street, Dennis Kearney's corner and running along said street in an Easterly direction sixty (60) feet; thence in a Northerly direction along the line of land of J. R. Mangum one hundred twenty (120) feet and three (3) inches; thence in a Westerly direction along the line of land of Will Debnam sixty (60) feet to a stake Dennis Kearney's line; thence in a Southerly direction along the line of land of Dennis Kearney one hundred twenty (120) feet and three (3) inches to the point of BEGINNING, Together with the right to the use of a well situated between the above described lot and a lot described as belonging to J. R. Mangum.

document_extractions.legal_match_notes: No parcel ID or plat reference available in this deed to confirm match to parcel 0029590. The deed describes a metes-and-bounds lot in Wake Forest, Wake County by adjoining landowners only. Cannot confirm or deny match without additional parcel data.
document_extractions.summary: Warranty deed dated August 1, 1944 from W. W. Holding and wife Josephine H. Holding to Lydia Hayes conveying a 60x120 ft lot on the North Side of Fort Street in Wake Forest, Wake County, NC; recorded November 6, 1944 in Book 913 at page 354. This document is the third of three instruments recorded on the same pages; the first two pages contain a separate deed from Kathryn Baker Cummings and husband Paul Cummings to D. A. Baker and wife Annie B. Baker recorded at Book 913 page 352.

document_extractions.references_prior_deed_language: secured by a Deed of Trust dated the same day as the Note in the original principal amount of Twenty-nine Thousand and no/100 Dollars ($29,000.00) by and among Borrower, Lender and Trustee (the "Deed of Trust") encumbering certain real property in Wake County, North Carolina as described therein (the "Property") and recorded in Book 6271, Page 345, Wake County Registry
document_extractions.legal_match_notes: No legal description is included in this document. The property is referenced only by citation to the prior Deed of Trust recorded at Book 6271, Page 345. document_extractions.summary: Modification Agreement for a Deferred Payment Loan dated April 13, 1995, between Borrower Lydia Hayes (widow), Trustee E. Richard Jones Jr., and Lender Wake County, modifying the original Promissory Note and Deed of Trust (Book 6271, Page 345) to increase the principal amount to $29,225.90 and revise the payment schedule; recorded at Book 6511, Page 265, Wake County Registry.

R. Mangum, formerly, now Viola Hicks, 120 feet and 3 inches; thence in a Westerly direction along line of land of Will Debnam, 60 feet to a stake, in Dennis Kearney's line; thence, in a Southerly direction, along line of the Kearney land, 120 feet 3 inches, to the point of BEGINNING.

document_extractions.summary: Quitclaim deed dated June 15, 1954, from Viola Hicks (widow) to Lydia Hayes, conveying a lot on the north side of Fort Street (now Nelson Street) in the Town of Wake Forest, Wake County, NC. The deed was executed to correct a mutual mistake in a prior 1941 deed (Book 860, Page 53) that inadvertently included this lot, which had already been separately conveyed to Lydia Hayes by deed recorded in Book 913, Page 354; recorded June 17, 1954 at Book 1155, Page 171.

document_extractions.summary: Warranty deed dated February 3, 1941 from W. W. Holding and wife Josephine A. Holding to Viola Hicks conveying Lots 19 and 19A in the Town of Wake Forest, Wake County, NC, described by metes and bounds; recorded July 11, 1941 in Book 860, Page 53.

investigation_questions.question: Current owner shows as 'HAYES, LYDIA HEIRS'. No acquisition deed into the heirs is recorded. I verified Lydia Hayes' acquisition by warranty deed from W. W. Holding (capture_id 2, extraction_id 1; Wake Book 913, Page 354, 11/6/1944) and confirmed the grantor’s standing via (a) Holding’s earlier 1941 deed describing adjacent/related lots (capture_id 4, extraction_id 4; Wake Book 860, Page 53) and (b) a corrective quitclaim from Viola Hicks back to Lydia clarifying the overlap (capture_id 3, extraction_id 3; Wake Book 1155, Page 171). However, I cannot locate any deed of distribution, executor’s deed, or heirship instrument transferring title from Lydia to her heirs. Searched Wake ROD name results and NC court portal for an estate for LYDIA HAYES (including variants) without success. Need probate or other instrument to establish how title moved from Lydia to her heirs.

deceased_signals.signal: owner appears as 'Lydia Hayes (widow)' in grantor_names

objections.problem: Acquisition type is 'deed' and chain_back_document is null. Flags 'chain_back_not_found', 'investigation_incomplete', and 'phases_bc_not_completed' are present, indicating the chain-back grantor deed (W. W. Holding's source of title prior to 1944) was not searched/retrieved. Investigation must complete chain-back search before this can be finalized.
objections.claim_being_challenged: chain_back_document is null for a deed acquisition

objections.problem: Primary extraction (Book 913 Page 354) has legal_match_to_parcel = 'low'. The conclusion has appropriately raised flag 'primary_acquisition_low_legal_match' and 'legal_description_low_confidence', but this remains a medium-severity concern: the deed cannot be definitively tied to parcel 0029590 without supplemental parcel evidence.

Extraction 3 (Book 1155 Page 171) is a quitclaim from Viola Hicks correcting a 1941 deed at Book 860 Page 53, NOT correcting the primary 1944 deed at Book 913 Page 354. Per the extraction summary, the 1941 deed (Book 860 Page 53) inadvertently included this lot which had already been conveyed to Lydia Hayes in 1944. Labeling extraction 3 as 'corrective' relative to the chain is misleading — it is a curative quitclaim resolving a cloud on title from the 1941 Holding-to-Hicks deed, not a correction of the primary deed. Role assignment should be re-evaluated.
objections.claim_being_challenged: Corrective/quitclaim deed (extraction 3) referenced as 'corrective' in supporting_documents

objections.problem: Extraction 2 grantor_names shows 'Lydia Hayes (widow)' as of April 13, 1995 — this confirms Lydia was a widow (her husband deceased), not that Lydia herself is deceased. The signal language as written ('Lydia Hayes (widow)') supports widowhood, not Lydia's own death. The deceased_confidence='confirmed' for Lydia Hayes is overclaimed; the only other signal ('name ends with HEIRS') is a name-pattern signal supporting probable but not confirmed death. Confidence should likely be 'probable' not 'confirmed'.
objections.claim_being_challenged: Deceased signal: 'owner appears as Lydia Hayes (widow) in grantor_names' cites extraction 2

objections.problem: The conclusion flags 'estate_path_unresolved' and lists Lydia Hayes as deceased with HEIRS, yet no probate/estate file or deed of distribution was retrieved or referenced. If Lydia is deceased, an estate search is required to determine if title passed by intestacy or testacy. This gap is acknowledged via the flag but should be resolved by completing the investigation.
objections.claim_being_challenged: estate_path_unresolved flag present but no estate document retrieved

verify_objections.reviewer_notes: Conclusion is structurally sound for the primary acquisition (1944 warranty deed Holding to Lydia Hayes, sole grantee) but has multiple unresolved investigation gaps appropriately flagged. Most critical issues are the missing chain-back deed, the unresolved estate path for the deceased owner, and the questionable role labeling of extraction 3 as 'corrective' (it actually corrects a separate 1941 deed in a parallel chain, not the primary). The deceased_confidence='confirmed' is also overclaimed given the cited evidence only proves widowhood, not Lydia's death. Returning to Investigate to complete chain-back and estate searches would resolve most issues.
